summary: Work around localhost exception issues in addUser helpers
description:
With MongoDB 2.6 the usersInfo command is used to determine if the addUser helper should call createUser or updateUser when passed a given username. Starting with MongoDB 2.7.0 the scope of the localhost exception for authentication was dramatically narrowed. It is no longer possible to call the userInfo command unauthenticated, even when the localhost exception is in effect. Any driver that calls usersInfo in its addUser helper can no longer be used to add the first admin user while the localhost exception is in effect:

{noformat}
>>> c = pymongo.MongoClient()
>>> try:
...     c.admin.add_user('admin', 'pass', roles=['root'])
... except Exception as exc:
...     print exc.details
... 
{u'code': 13, u'ok': 0.0, u'errmsg': u'not authorized on admin to execute command { usersInfo: "admin" }'}
>>> 
>>> c.admin.command('createUser', 'admin', pwd='pass', roles=['root'])
{u'ok': 1.0}
>>> c.admin.authenticate('admin', 'pass')
True
>>> c.server_info()['version']
u'2.7.2'
{noformat}

A workaround for this issue is to catch the exception and call createUser if the error code is 13 (Unauthorized). Any exception from the createUser call should propagate to the user application.

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blob/master/src/mongo/base/error_codes.err

summary: DBCursor is missing a limit getter method
description: DBCursor should expose the _limit field with a getLimit() method.

summary: Use UUIDCodec from registry in DBObjectCodec
description: DBObject should respect the UUIDCodec that's in the registry, so that users can control the UUID representation when encoding/decoding using DBObject

summary: Remove LazyWriteableDBObject and related classes
description:
The LazyWriteableDBObject class is dangerous as it violates the contract of it's superclass.  If you attempt to encode a LazyWriteableDBObject with the LazyDBObjectEncoder, any modifications that were made to the LazyWriteableDBObject will be ignored.

For this reason we will deprecate the class and remove it in 3.0.

summary: Deprecate LazyWriteableDBObject and related classes
description:
The LazyWriteableDBObject class is dangerous as it violates the contract of it's superclass.  If you attempt to encode a LazyWriteableDBObject with the LazyDBObjectEncoder, any modifications that were made to the LazyWriteableDBObject will be ignored.

For this reason we will deprecate the class and remove it in 3.0.

summary: Deprecate DBCollection.apply/doApply
description: In the 3.0 design these methods are no longer even called, so they need to be deprecated in 2.13 and removed in 3.0

summary: Removed DBCollection.apply/doapply methods
description: These methods are no longer even called, so they should be removed so as not to mislead users.

summary: Document "Lazy" classes
description:
The following classes need updated Javadoc:

 - LazyBSONCallback
 - LazyBSONObject
 - LazyBSONList
 - LazyDBCallback
 - LazyDBDecoder
 - LazyDBList
 - LazyDBObject
 - LazyWriteableDBCallback
 - LazyWriteableDBDecoder
 - LazyWriteableDBObject

summary: ByteBuf should be reference counted instead of Closeable
Default MongoCollectionOptions
description:
You should be able to create MongoCollectionOptions with the appropriate default values. In other words

MongoDatabase db = ...
db.getCollection("my-col", MongoCollectionOptions.builder().build())

should be equiv to

MongoDatabase db = ...
db.getCollection("my-col");

summary: Handle DBRef encoding and decoding in Document Codec
description: Like DBObjectCodec, DocumentCodec should be able to encode from and decode to a DBRef.

summary: unordered bulk writes should continue sending batches after an error
description:
The bulk write API added in 2.12 supports sending inserts, updates, and deletes in bulk to the server.  If the number of writes requests exceeds 1000, or the total size of the write requests exceed 16MB, the driver will split the write requests into batches of 1000 and/or batches whose size does not exceed 16MB.

For ordered bulk writes, the driver stops sending more batches to the server if there were any errors reported in the current batch.

For unordered bulk writes, the driver should keep sending the rest of the batches to the server, even if errors were reported in the current batch.  There is a bug which is preventing that from happening.  Instead, the driver stops sending more batches and immediately reports the first batch of errors to the application, ignoring any subsequent batches.

An application will be effected by this bug in the following circumstances:

* It is using the method DBCollection.initializeUnorderedBulkWriteOperation
* The number of writes added to the operation exceeds 1000 or 16MB of total data
* The possibility of an error (most likely a duplicate key error) exists


Workaround:

Limit the number of writes in a single bulk write operation to less than 1000 and less than 16 MB

summary: Deprecate DB.requestStart, DB.requestDone, DB.requestEnsureConnection
description: The main use case for these methods is to ensure that applications can read their own unacknowledged writes, but this is no longer so prevalent since the driver started defaulting to acknowledged writes. The other main use case is to ensure that related read operations are all routed to the same server when using a non-primary read preference.  But this is dangerous because mongos no longer provides this guarantee.  For these reasons, this method is now deprecated and will be removed in the next major release.

summary: Receive/Send Buffer Size not set appropriately
description: The receive and send buffer sizes from SocketSettings not applied correctly in AsynchronousSocketChannelStream & NettyStream

summary: Remove DB.requestStart, DB.requestDone, DB.requestEnsureConnection
description: The main use case for these methods is to ensure that applications can read their own unacknowledged writes, but this is no longer so prevalent since the driver started defaulting to acknowledged writes. The other main use case is to ensure that related read operations are all routed to the same server when using a non-primary read preference. But this is dangerous because mongos no longer provides this guarantee. For these reasons, these methods will be removed.

summary: SingleConnectionBinding should share a connection if the readPreference is primary
description: Currently SingleConnectionBinding has two connections one for reads and one for writes.  If the readPreference is primary they should share a connection.

summary: Cannot authenticate against 2.8.0.rc-0.
Operations that return cursors should not abstract batches of results
description: It will be useful to high level APIs if operations that return cursors expose the batches as first class citizens.  This will allow batch size to be controlled on a per-request basis, which will help create reactive streams that support back pressure.

summary: Allow getIndexes to return a list of a provided class
allow executeCommand to return the type of a provided class
Explain should set limit to the absolute value of the limit * -1
description: Still investigating why this should be so, but the 2.x Java driver does it, as does the shell.

summary: Operations not checking if WriteConcernResult was acknowledged
description: Inside MongoCollectionImpl when creating an UpdateResult for example, the code does not check if the WriteConcernResult was acknowledged or not, which throws error when it's an unacknowledged write.

summary: WriteConcernResult should be an abstract class
Add independent setting for timeout when selecting a server to operate against
description:
In 2.x, we use connectTimeout, which overloads the meaning (since we also use it for Socket connect timeout),

In 3.x, it's not configurable yet, but there's an easy place to hook a value in, in BaseCluster.getServer().

Possible name: serverSelectionTimeout

summary: MongoClient should implement Closeable
description: MongoClient (or even Mongo) object should implement the Closeable interface, in order to be able to use it in a try-with-resources clause.

summary: Async ConnectionInitializer
description: Make the {{PipelinedConnectionInitializer.initialize( initializationFuture )}} non-blocking.

summary: Get rid of MongoFuture
description: In core, just use SingleResultCallback.  In driver-async, overload methods, one that takes a SingleResultCallback, the other which does not and returns a regular Future.

summary: async operation execution is leaking connections
Driver throws IllegalStateException unnecessarily
description:
The driver in some cases will throw an IllegalStateException indicating that the MongoClient has been closed, even in situations where it's not necessary. For example, the check is made in DBTCPConnector.releasePort, which is called in a number of places after an operation is completed.  

It would be better just to allow those in-flight operations to complete normally and not throw.

summary: ByteBuf leaked on exception in Stream implementations
description:
ByteBuf is leaked on exception in the following Stream implementations:

* SocketStream
* SocketChannelStream
* AsynchronousSocketChannelStream

summary: Make driver module compliant with checkstyle.xml
description: Currently it uses checkstyle-lite.xml

summary: Add Stream.open/openAsync methods
description: To go along with InternalStreamConnection

description:
Getting the following stacktrace (operation still succeeds) when calling FindFluent#first

summary: Support storageEngine field for create collection/indexes helpers and operations
description: MongoDB 2.8 added support for a storageEngine field to the createCollection and createIndexes commands.  The value is a map with unspecified schema.  The driver should pass this map through to the commands.

summary: getCollectionNames returning null elements
description: Calling getCollectionNames is returning null elements in the list.

summary: FindFluent#first throws exception when no results are returned
description: Getting IndexOutOfBoundsException when I call FindFluent#first if no results are returned as part of the query.

summary: Handle asynchronously delivered exceptions in connection pool
description: DefaultConnectionPool catches and re-throws exceptions from the wrapped connection for synchronous calls, incrementing the generation count.  But it does not for sendMessageAsync or receiveMessageAsync.

summary: Support encoding embedded Map within Document
description:
Given 

{code:java}
Map myJson = ... 
Document doc = new Document(myJson)
{code}

and myJson contains a value which is itself a Map, encoding fails:

{noformat}
org.bson.codecs.configuration.CodecConfigurationException: Can't find a codec for class java.util.LinkedHashMap.
    at org.bson.codecs.configuration.RootCodecRegistry.get(RootCodecRegistry.java:74)
    at org.bson.codecs.configuration.RootCodecRegistry.get(RootCodecRegistry.java:60)
    at com.mongodb.codecs.DocumentCodec.writeValue(DocumentCodec.java:168)
    at com.mongodb.codecs.DocumentCodec.encode(DocumentCodec.java:127)
    at com.mongodb.codecs.DocumentCodec.encode(DocumentCodec.java:44)
    at com.mongodb.protocol.message.RequestMessage.addDocument(RequestMessage.java:111)
    at com.mongodb.protocol.message.RequestMessage.addCollectibleDocument(RequestMessage.java:102)
{noformat}

summary: Add new exception types for writes from the new CRUD API
description:
* MongoWriteException
* MongoWriteConcernException

summary: Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes commands
description: In order to support listing collections of databases with a large number of collections, the listCollections command that was introduced in 2.8 will need to be able to return a cursor. Otherwise, it will run into the 16mb document size limit.

summary: Handle asynchronous aggregate/mapreduce to a collection
description: There is currently a race condition because the aggregate/mapreduce is asynchronously executed but then a MongoIterable on the collection the results are being written to is returned immediately

summary: Write operations in async MongoCollection not translating exceptions properly
description: The callback which is translating the MongoBulkWriteException is throwing the translated exception instead of calling the user callback with it.  And it's ignoring all other exceptions.

summary: DialInfo api prevents tls hostname verification
Drivers should not allow sending _ids with a key starting with $
mgo driver panic on mutex state when under significant load
description:
While performing an intense load test of MMS's go monitoring agent, the agent encountered a panic with the following trace:

{noformat}
panic: sync: unlock of unlocked mutex

goroutine 1244643 [running]:
runtime.panic(0x68aa40, 0xc2259430b0)
        /usr/local/go/src/pkg/runtime/panic.c:266 +0xb6
sync.(*Mutex).Unlock(0xc21775d8a8)
        /usr/local/go/src/pkg/sync/mutex.go:91 +0x98
labix.org/v2/mgo.func·011(0x0, 0x0, 0xc21e554700, 0x0, 0xc21ed57000, ...)
        /mnt/jenkins/workspace/Monitoring-Agent-RPM/build/build/go-dependancies/src/labix.org/v2/mgo/socket.go:321 +0x5e
labix.org/v2/mgo.(*mongoSocket).readLoop(0xc2129079a0)
        /mnt/jenkins/workspace/Monitoring-Agent-RPM/build/build/go-dependancies/src/labix.org/v2/mgo/socket.go:573 +0xe04
created by labix.org/v2/mgo.newSocket
        /mnt/jenkins/workspace/Monitoring-Agent-RPM/build/build/go-dependancies/src/labix.org/v2/mgo/socket.go:163 +0x2b3
...
{noformat}

Note: to be fair, this was _meant_ to be a load test, and therefore I took some steps to purposely apply extra strain and concurrency on the agent and underlying MongoDB servers to which mgo was connecting:

1.) The MongoDB servers were underspec'ed; the machines were m1.large's with 5 MongoDB hosts per server.
2.) This MongoDB deployment being monitored was _not_ idle; they were serving a fair amount of application load and _did_ experience some connectivity issues/timeouts/failovers within the cluster.
3.) The go monitoring agent program was running on a relatively small machine: m1.small (one step up from micro).

Unclear if perhaps another data point for the following:
https://bugs.launchpad.net/mgo/+bug/1225196
(The panic message is the same, but the trace line numbers are different, which could of course simply be mgo version differences.)

CC [~cailin.nelson@10gen.com]

summary: Down replica set member using Direct causes high CPU spin loop
description:
Given a simple reproducer program (attached) that makes {{Direct}} connections to MongoDB 2.6 PSA (primary/secondary/arbiter) replica set members, shutting down the secondary causes a tight spin loop of trying to reconnect. This loop appears to repeat without a delay, consuming high CPU (~150%) and large number of {{pinger}} goroutines.

*Steps to reproduce*

{code}
# First add "127.0.0.1 foo" entry to /etc/hosts

# Commands used to startup the replica set
mongod --fork --replSet agent-rs --port 30000 --dbpath data/agent-rs-0/ --logpath logs/agent-rs-0/mongod.log --smallfiles --nojournal --nohttpinterface
mongod --fork --replSet agent-rs --port 30001 --dbpath data/agent-rs-1/ --logpath logs/agent-rs-1/mongod.log --smallfiles --nojournal --nohttpinterface
mongod --fork --replSet agent-rs --port 30002 --dbpath data/agent-rs-2/ --logpath logs/agent-rs-2/mongod.log --smallfiles --nojournal --nohttpinterface

mongo --port 30000
> rs.initiate()
> rs.add("foo:30001")
> rs.addArb("foo:30002")
{code}

Then {{kill -2 \[pid-of-30001-secondary\]}}

Repeated log statements from reproducer with debug logging enabled:

{noformat}
...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Synchronization was partial (cannot talk to primary).
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Synchronization completed: 0 master(s) and 0 slave(s) alive.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Cluster 0xc2100531e0 released (refs=1)
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC No masters found. Will synchronize again.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Cluster 0xc2100531e0 is starting a sync loop iteration.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Starting full topology synchronization...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Address foo:30001 resolved as 127.0.0.1:30001
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Establishing new connection to foo:30001 (timeout=15s)...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Connection to foo:30001 failed: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:30001: connection refused
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Processing foo:30001...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Establishing new connection to foo:30001 (timeout=5s)...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Connection to foo:30001 failed: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:30001: connection refused
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Failed to get socket to foo:30001: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:30001: connection refused
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Connections to foo:30001 closing (0 live sockets).
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Synchronization was partial (cannot talk to primary).
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Synchronization completed: 0 master(s) and 0 slave(s) alive.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Cluster 0xc2100531e0 released (refs=1)
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC No masters found. Will synchronize again.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Cluster 0xc2100531e0 is starting a sync loop iteration.
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Starting full topology synchronization...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Address foo:30001 resolved as 127.0.0.1:30001
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Establishing new connection to foo:30001 (timeout=15s)...
2014/04/28 03:21:22 Connection to foo:30001 failed: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:30001: connection refused
2014/04/28 03:21:22 SYNC Processing foo:30001...
...
{noformat}

Note: while the above details describe the exact setup, it's not clear if all the environment details are required. E.g., the MongoDB being version 2.6 and the hosts being local to the application.

summary: Upserting a user from Go into "admin" gives "upsert query needs _id"
Support 'serviceHostname' auth param for Kerberos
description: As per convo over email, DialInfo should have a way of passing 'serviceHostname' to the server. Should involve just adding a single bson field, see use of saslCommandServiceHostnameFieldName in https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/commit/f18e88ffafd615d515f3359ee73f719b1667e193

summary: Add "How to Ask for Help" section to Github readme
